thinking.text:
The pair-based method works great! The detected breakpoints match the true ones very closely. But I notice that neurons 16 and 19 have very close t values (-0.0339 and -0.0327) and their |t*Δ| values are close (0.0003067 and 0.0002860). These might be incorrectly paired or grouped.

Also, I see that the detected Δ values have wrong sign for some neurons (e.g., neuron 6 detected Δ=+0.00105 but true Δ should be? Let me check: A1[6,0] = -0.067246, A2[6] = 0.015651. Since A1[i,j] < 0, the ReLU turns OFF at t, so the slope decreases. The slope before is A2[i]*A1[i,j] (negative * positive = negative), after is 0. So Δ = -A2[i]*A1[i,j] = -0.015651*(-0.067246) = +0.00105. That matches. Good.

But for clustering I should use |t*Δ| since the sign of t*Δ depends on the sign of A1[i,j] (since Δ = A2[i]*|A1[i,j]| has sign of A2[i], and t = -b1/A1 has sign depending on both). Actually let me re-derive:

Δ = A2[i] * |A1[i,j]|
t = -b1[i] / A1[i,j]
t * Δ = -b1[i] * A2[i] * |A1[i,j]| / A1[i,j] = -b1[i] * A2[i] * sign(A1[i,j])

So |t * Δ| = |b1[i] * A2[i]| which is constant per neuron. Good.

The pair-based method gives precise t values. Now I need to run it for all 10 directions, cluster by |t*Δ|, and recover A1.

Let me run the full script.
